message.entry: Duration must be paired with duration inputs.
Unable to combine a datetime that has a time zone with an unzoned datetime.
Durations may only be used when specifying interval type. Use datetimes when specifying unit of time.
{0} is not a valid input argument. Call TIMERANGE with two datetimes or two durations, and use output as a subscript for a timetable.
Arguments must be scalars.
Ambiguous value 'm' for unit of time. Do you mean: ''{0}'' or ''{1}''?
Specify third argument either as interval type ('openright', 'openleft', 'open', 'closed') or as unit of time ('years', 'quarters', 'months', 'weeks', 'days', 'hours', 'minutes', 'seconds').
Subscripting using TIMERANGE is only supported for selecting rows of a timetable.
TIMERANGE endpoints must be datetime or duration scalars, or text representing datetime or duration scalars.
A timetable with {0} row times cannot be subscripted using {1} values.

entry.note: Do NOT add period ('.') at the end - it is intentionally left out.

message.entry: Unable to load the saved timerange object because it is incompatible with this version of MATLAB

entry.note: Do NOT translate 'timetable' and 'timerange': they are class names. 'subscript' here means an array index.

message.entry: timetable timerange subscript:
Select timetable rows with times in the open interval:\n\t\t({0}, {1})
Select timetable rows with times in the closed interval:\n\t\t[{0}, {1}]
Select timetable rows with times in the half-open interval:\n\t\t({0}, {1}]
Select timetable rows with times in the half-open interval:\n\t\t[{0}, {1})
Select timetable rows with times in: {0}\n\t\t Starting at, including: {1}\n\t\t Ending at, but excluding: {2}
Select no rows because an endpoint is a {0}.
Select no rows because the left endpoint is at or after the right endpoint.

entry.note: Do NOT translate 'timetable': it is a class name

message.entry: See Select Timetable Data by Row Time and Variable Type.
Code generation does not support specifying TIMERANGE endpoints as text. Specify duration endpoints instead.
Interval type and unit of time input must be constant.
Code generation does not support specifying unit of time in TIMERANGE.
